name: Homepage Enterprise Inquiry & Book-Demo Widgets
description: Embedded homepage forms letting a prospect submit an enterprise sales inquiry (ICP-scored) or book a live demo, without leaving the landing page.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09. Both root causes corrected: (1) p4CreateLead() now sends {name,email,company,role,sector,company_size,budget_range,urgency,phone,message} matching handleCreateLead's real required/optional fields exactly (was {contact_name,contact_email,company_name,...}); p4BookDemo() now sends preferred_slot (was slot_id). (2) The shared p4Api() helper (frontend/index.html, Phase 4 Revenue Engine IIFE) now unwraps the {success,data,error,timestamp} envelope, flattening data's fields to the top level while preserving success/error/code — fixing this capability plus CAP-CRM-005 and CAP-AFF-001 in one change, since all three share the same helper. Success-check branches updated to the real flat field names (lead_id/icp_score/priority; booked/booking_id/slot/meet_link — the old code checked d.lead and d.booking.booking_ref, neither of which ever existed at any level of a real response). A third, previously-uncatalogued bug in the same flow was also found and fixed: p4LoadDemoSlots()'s slot picker rendered s.slot_id/s.display, but GET /api/sales/demo/slots actually returns {slot,label} — meaning even a field-name-correct booking submission would have sent an empty preferred_slot (blank radio value) forever; now reads s.slot/s.label. Playwright-verified end-to-end: lead submit sends the real field names and renders the real ICP score/priority; demo slot picker shows a real, non-blank slot value; demo booking sends preferred_slot and renders a real confirmation message.

navigation.evidence: Both forms are embedded directly in frontend/index.html, the platform's primary landing page.

test_coverage.evidence: Repo-wide search for an import from workers/src/handlers/salesPipeline.js under workers/test/**/*.mjs still returns zero direct-import matches. Verified this pass via an ad-hoc Playwright click-through (not committed to the repo); a real gap, flagged as a follow-up rather than fixed in this pass.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/salesPipeline.js re-read to confirm exact required/optional field names and response shapes. Playwright click-through of the real frontend/index.html (mocked network responses shaped exactly to the handler's real return values) confirmed both forms now send correct field names and render correct success messages using real response fields. Full backend suite (188 files/2012 tests) green — this was a frontend-only change plus the shared envelope fix. NOT YET verified against the live deployed backend — customer_journey_complete stays false pending a post-merge production smoke test with real lead/demo submissions.

notes: Two independent, revenue-critical top-of-funnel forms on the primary landing page previously failed 100% of submissions. Fixed 2026-07-09: field names, response-envelope unwrap (shared with CAP-CRM-005/CAP-AFF-001), and a third latent slot-picker bug found during the fix (see frontend.evidence). See CAP-CRM-003 for a working alternative that reaches the same backend correctly, unaffected by any of this.

name: Dedicated Booking Page (Lead Capture + Demo Booking)
description: A separate, dedicated booking page that correctly captures a sales lead and books a demo slot against the same backend the homepage widgets (CAP-CRM-001) fail to reach correctly.

frontend.evidence: frontend/booking.html:1396-1411 posts to /api/sales/leads with name/email/company/phone/role/sector — matching workers/src/handlers/salesPipeline.js:106's required fields exactly — and :1420-1430 posts to /api/sales/demo/book with preferred_slot, matching handleBookDemo's requirement. Both check response.ok and the real flat field names (leadData.lead_id) rather than a nonexistent nested key. frontend/booking.html:1382-1392 contains a comment documenting that this page previously had the same class of bug (posting to /api/leads/capture with a workEmail field the handler didn't read) and was fixed to call the CRM routes directly with matching field names — verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: Not established in this pass whether frontend/booking.html is linked from primary navigation or only reachable via direct URL/campaign links — needs a follow-up nav-crawl check.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file imports workers/src/handlers/salesPipeline.js directly (see CAP-CRM-001); this working page has no direct regression coverage either, despite being the only currently-functional caller of these two routes.

verification.evidence: frontend/booking.html:1382-1430 read directly and cross-checked field-by-field against workers/src/handlers/salesPipeline.js:100-111,340-348.

notes: priority P3 reflects the unresolved navigation-discoverability question, not a functional defect — the page itself works correctly. Marking navigation.discoverable as 'unknown' rather than guessing true/false, per this registry's evidence discipline; a dynamic_browser nav-crawl would resolve this in one pass.

name: Customer Personalized Risk Radar & Asset Inventory
description: Authenticated customer-facing dashboard showing a personalized risk radar, organization risk posture, and a self-managed asset inventory (register/list/delete monitored assets).

frontend.evidence: frontend/customer-dashboard.html calls all 8 exported routes with matching field names — e.g. asset registration POST body {asset_type,asset_value,label} (:585) matches workers/src/handlers/customerIntel.js:478-482 exactly. Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: Not established in this pass whether frontend/customer-dashboard.html is linked from primary navigation or from the main frontend/user-dashboard.html — needs a follow-up nav-crawl check; these are two separate pages that were not compared for overlap/duplication in this pass.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file imports workers/src/handlers/customerIntel.js directly.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/customerIntel.js exports enumerated; frontend/customer-dashboard.html cross-checked against all 8 routes.

notes: Whether this page duplicates or complements frontend/user-dashboard.html's own scan-history/risk features is an open question worth a follow-up pass — flagging rather than asserting either way.

name: Customer Success Health Scoring
description: Backend health-score computation for customer-success tracking (churn risk, engagement) — distinct from the similarly-named CAP-CRM-005 capability that is actually wired to the frontend page named for this concept.

frontend.evidence: Repo-wide search (all frontend file types, not just .html) for any /api/customer-success/* path returns zero matches. frontend/customer-success-dashboard.html — despite its name closely matching this handler's route prefix — does not call it at all; it calls /api/customer/success/score (frontend/customer-success-dashboard.html:547), which workers/src/index.js:5432-5434 routes to a completely different file, workers/src/handlers/commercialPlatformHandler.js's handleCustomerSuccessScore. Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: No nav entry calls this handler's routes because no page does.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file imports workers/src/handlers/customerSuccess.js directly. workers/test/authRoleDerivation.test.mjs mentions the file only in a header comment describing a previously-fixed, unrelated authCtx.role bug — it does not exercise this handler's actual endpoints.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/customerSuccess.js exports and route registrations read directly; frontend/customer-success-dashboard.html read directly and confirmed to call a different handler entirely.

notes: backend.status is "duplicate" because a real, currently-used 'customer success score' concept already exists and is wired (commercialPlatformHandler.js's handleCustomerSuccessScore, consumed by customer-success-dashboard.html) — this file appears to be an earlier or parallel implementation of the same idea that was never connected to any UI. Worth a reconciliation decision (retire one, or merge) rather than building a UI for this one on top of an already-served concept.

name: Proposal Generation
description: Generate a branded sales proposal document for a prospect from a service package and lead context.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09 for the frontend/index.html widget (this is an owner-only tool by design: data-auth-gate="owner" hides the section for everyone but the owner, consistent with the backend's isOwner gate below — 'public-facing' in the original evidence referred to its location on the public homepage's DOM, not its reachability). p4GenerateProposal() previously sent {client_company,client_contact,client_email,sector,org_size,package_id,challenges} but handleGenerateProposal requires lead_id (workers/src/handlers/proposalGenerator.js:326,331, MISSING_LEAD) with no lead-selection field anywhere in the form. Since buildProposalDocument reads client.name/company/email/phone/sector/company_size/role straight from the stored lead record — exactly the fields handleCreateLead already populates from matching form fields — the fix chains the two already-fixed real endpoints: p4GenerateProposal() now POSTs to /api/sales/leads first (creating/attaching a lead from the form's own company/contact/email/sector/size fields), then POSTs to /api/proposals/generate with the returned lead_id + package_id + notes. Success/list rendering fixed to the real flat response fields (proposal.proposal_number, proposal.pricing.total_with_gst_inr, proposal.roi_analysis.roi_multiplier — these were already correct in the old code; only the envelope unwrap, shared with CAP-CRM-001/CAP-AFF-001, was needed there). p4LoadProposals() was reading p.client_company/p.proposal_number/p.total_inr/p.roi_multiplier; handleListProposals's real D1 row shape is {company, package_id, status, value_inr, metadata:{proposal_number}} with no per-row ROI figure at all (removed rather than showing a permanent fake value) — now reads p.company/p.metadata.proposal_number/p.value_inr. A separate bug found in the same pass: the 'Proposal History' panel's '↻ Refresh' button used onclick="p4LoadProposals()", but that function had no window. prefix (IIFE-private) — a real user click would have thrown ReferenceError; now window.p4LoadProposals. frontend/proposal-generator.html (the separate staff-internal, entirely client-side reimplementation) is unchanged — real duplicate effort, still worth a deliberate keep-one decision, not addressed in this pass.

navigation.evidence: frontend/proposal-generator.html is linked from frontend/revenue-command-center.html:501 and frontend/mssp-command-center.html:541 (both staff-only pages); the broken homepage widget is embedded directly in frontend/index.html.

permissions: isOwner (proposals routes gated owner-only per workers/src/index.js)

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/proposalBola.test.mjs and workers/test/proposalGeneratorHonestClaims.test.mjs unchanged, still cover the handler in isolation. The frontend chain (lead-create then generate) was verified this pass via an ad-hoc Playwright click-through (not committed to the repo) rather than a permanent regression test — a real gap, flagged as a follow-up.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/proposalGenerator.js re-read (buildProposalDocument's lead-field reads, handleGenerateProposal's required lead_id, handleListProposals's real D1 row shape). Playwright click-through of the real frontend/index.html (mocked lead-create then mocked proposal-generate, both shaped exactly to the real handlers) confirmed the two-step chain fires correctly, the success message renders the real proposal number/pricing/ROI, and the history list renders real company/proposal-number/value fields. Full backend suite (188 files/2012 tests) green — this was a frontend-only change. NOT YET verified against the live deployed backend — customer_journey_complete stays false pending a post-merge production smoke test by the owner (the only real user of this gated tool).

notes: A working, tested backend API for proposal generation exists; the frontend/index.html owner-only widget now correctly chains lead-creation into proposal-generation and renders real response data (fixed 2026-07-09, including a separate ReferenceError-on-click bug in its own Refresh button). frontend/proposal-generator.html — a separate, staff-internal, entirely client-side reimplementation — is unchanged; real duplicate effort, still worth a deliberate keep-one decision, not addressed in this pass.

name: Security Assessment Booking
description: Dedicated booking flow for the ₹9,999 Security Assessment service package, with plan definitions, confirmation, and status tracking.

frontend.evidence: Repo-wide search (all file types) for /api/assessments returns zero matches anywhere in frontend/. The homepage's 'Book a Free Consultation' form has a package option literally priced 'Security Assessment — ₹9,999' (frontend/index.html:6432) that happens to match ASSESSMENT_PLANS.standard.price_inr = 9999 in workers/src/handlers/assessmentBooking.js:27, but that form's submit handler, submitEnterpriseV10(), posts to POST /api/enterprise/book (frontend/index.html:6853), which workers/src/index.js:6302-6305 routes to a different file entirely, handleBookConsultation in workers/src/handlers/enterpriseLayer.js. Verified 2026-07-08 — this handler has no live caller.

navigation.evidence: No nav entry calls this handler's routes because no page does.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file imports workers/src/handlers/assessmentBooking.js directly.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/assessmentBooking.js read directly; frontend/index.html:6420-6853 read directly and traced to confirm the actual submit path goes to a different handler.

notes: backend.status is "duplicate": the real, currently-booked Security Assessment flow is handleBookConsultation in enterpriseLayer.js, not this file. This appears to be an earlier or parallel implementation that was superseded without being removed.

name: Conversion Trigger & Funnel Tracking
description: Behavioral event tracking and dynamic upsell/paywall/CTA triggers driven by customer usage patterns.

frontend.evidence: frontend/index.html calls all 6 of this handler's routes (:20890,20913,20949,20972,20988,21006) via the same unwrapped p4Post/p4Get helper described in CAP-CRM-001, so the envelope-mismatch bug applies here too. Additionally, several parameter names don't match: the frontend sends event_type (:20890) but handleRecordEvent reads event (workers/src/handlers/conversionTriggers.js:235); several calls send user_id but the backend only ever reads authCtx?.userId or a session_id field/query param, never user_id (workers/src/handlers/conversionTriggers.js:236,267,335); the paywall call sends feature_id (:20972) but handleGetPaywall reads a feature query param and 400s without it (workers/src/handlers/conversionTriggers.js:297,300). The CTA call's plan/context params (:20988) do match the backend exactly (workers/src/handlers/conversionTriggers.js:381-382), so that one path likely works modulo the envelope bug.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/conversionTriggers.js required-field reads compared line-by-line against frontend/index.html's 6 call sites.

notes: Marked partial rather than fully broken because at least one path (CTA) has matching field names. UPDATE 2026-07-09: the shared p4Api() envelope-unwrap bug common to all 6 routes was fixed as part of the CAP-CRM-001/CAP-CRM-005/CAP-AFF-001 change (same helper). This capability's own separate, per-endpoint field-name mismatches (event_type-vs-event, user_id-vs-authCtx, feature_id-vs-feature) were NOT in scope for that fix and remain unverified/unaddressed — operational_status and priority intentionally left unchanged pending a dedicated pass on this capability specifically. The CTA path (already field-name-correct) should now work end-to-end; the other paths still need their field names fixed.

name: Growth & Revenue Automation Suite
description: 27-function backend covering email capture, sales-pipeline automation, outreach, content automation, drip campaigns, API-key provisioning, billing callbacks, revenue dashboards, upsell tracking, and LinkedIn automation.

frontend.evidence: Repo-wide search across every file under frontend/ (all file types) for any /api/growth/* path, or /api/billing/callback, or /api/billing/create-link, returns zero matches. All 27 exported functions — including customer-facing-sounding ones (pricing, feature walls, API-key provisioning, revenue dashboard) — currently have zero frontend callers. Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: No nav entry calls any of this handler's routes because no page does.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file imports workers/src/handlers/growth.js directly. workers/test/growthSalesOutreach.test.mjs imports markOutreachSent from a different file (workers/src/services/salesEngine.js) and exercises this handler's routes only indirectly by importing the full app router (workers/src/index.js) — not a direct unit test of this handler.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/growth.js's 27 exports enumerated; workers/src/index.js:4063-4392 grepped for /api/growth/* registrations; frontend/ searched exhaustively for any caller.

notes: The single largest backend-only orphan surface found in this domain by function count. auth_enforced/rbac.enforced marked unknown rather than asserted — with 27 functions, individually confirming each one's gate was out of scope for this pass; flagged honestly rather than assumed.
